notes: Two turns separated by a /model slash-command. MergeMetrics' 'latest non-empty/non-unknown wins' rule (session.go L627-628) promotes the new model name after turn 2; the recording's events.jsonl shows turn 1 attributed to model A (PerTurnContribution.Model=A) and turn 2 attributed to model B (PerTurnContribution.Model=B), and SessionMetrics.ModelName flips to B by t2_end. TotalTokens accumulates across both models (T2 > T1). The /model command itself produces a <local-command> wrapper that the parser filters via isLocalCommandContent — no state machinery fires during the switch window. The model_switch_window phase asserts that gap with duration_at_least_ms and a 'no working transition' invariant. Driver caveat: /model in claudecode is interactive; the recipe may need to drive the picker via send-keys (see recipe by_adapter.claudecode.script). KNOWN-FAILING (issue #405): Recording currently fails phase t2_start because the tmux /model picker doesn't deterministically select model B; t2_end then cascades with 'unknown anchor t2_start'. Awaiting a robust /model driver primitive — daemon observability is fine, capture is the gap.

text: Turn 1 user prompt → working under model A
Turn 1 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model A
/model slash command runs locally; <local-command> content is filtered; state stays ready, no working flicker
Turn 2 user prompt → working under model B
Turn 2 completes back to ready; SessionMetrics.ModelName = model B (latest wins); TotalTokens after T2 > TotalTokens after T1
